# agentpassport

> The AI passport layer. Cryptographic identity and delegation for AI agents.

## What It Is

agentpassport gives AI agents a cryptographic identity and a provable authorization chain. When Agent A delegates work to Agent B, any verifier can prove what Agent B was authorized to do — across languages, frameworks, and trust boundaries.

## Install

```bash
pip install agentpassport        # Python
npm install @agentpassport/core  # TypeScript
```

## Core Concepts

- **Identity:** Every agent has an Ed25519 keypair and a `did:key:` DID. No central registry.
- **Delegation:** Signed JWTs form an auth chain. Scope can only narrow at each hop — never expand.
- **Enforcement:** `verify_auth_chain()` and `TrustMiddleware` check before execution. Fail fast, no partial state.
- **Revocation:** Soft-stop by JTI. In-flight actions complete; next action is blocked.
- **Ownership binding:** Agents can prove domain or wallet ownership via `/.well-known/agent-passport.json`.

## Key Functions

### Python

```python
# Identity
generate_keypair() -> (private_key, public_key)
did_from_public_key(public_key) -> "did:key:z6Mk..."

# Delegation
sign_delegation(issuer_private_key, issuer_did, subject_did, scope, ttl_seconds) -> token
verify_auth_chain(auth_chain, expected_subject, known_public_keys) -> bool

# Ownership binding
bind_domain(private_key, agent_did, domain) -> Binding
bind_wallet(private_key, agent_did, chain, address) -> Binding
revoke_wallet(private_key, agent_did, chain, address) -> Revocation
verify_domain_binding(agent_did, domain) -> bool
verify_wallet_binding(agent_did, chain, address, domain) -> bool

# Revocation
InMemoryRevocationRegistry()
SqliteRevocationRegistry(db_path)
```

### TypeScript

```typescript
generateKeypair() -> { privateKey, publicKey }
didFromPublicKey(publicKey) -> "did:key:z6Mk..."
signDelegation({ issuerPrivateKey, issuerDid, subjectDid, scope, ttlSeconds }) -> token
verifyAuthChain({ authChain, expectedSubject, knownPublicKeys }) -> boolean
```

## Wire Format

Delegation tokens are compact EdDSA JWTs (Ed25519). Python and TypeScript SDKs are wire-compatible — a token signed by one can be verified by the other.

## Ownership Binding

Agents can prove real-world ownership by publishing a `BindingDocument` at `/.well-known/agent-passport.json`:

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "bindings": [
    {
      "type": "domain",
      "agent_did": "did:key:z6Mk...",
      "claim": { "domain": "agentpassport.fyi" },
      "issued_at": 1778716471,
      "expires_at": null,
      "signature": "<ed25519 hex>"
    },
    {
      "type": "wallet",
      "agent_did": "did:key:z6Mk...",
      "claim": { "chain": "ethereum", "address": "0x1234..." },
      "issued_at": 1778716471,
      "expires_at": null,
      "signature": "<ed25519 hex>"
    }
  ],
  "revocations": []
}
```

## Packages

- `agentpassport` — Python core SDK
- `@agentpassport/core` — TypeScript SDK
- `agentpassport-registry` — Trusted agent registry (FastAPI)
- `agentpassport-adapters` — MCP, REST, A2A adapters
- `agentpassport-cli` — CLI tooling

## Live Domain

The canonical domain for agentpassport is `agentpassport.fyi`. The ownership binding document is published at:

```
https://agentpassport.fyi/.well-known/agent-passport.json
```

This endpoint demonstrates a live domain binding and can be used to verify that the project DID is bound to `agentpassport.fyi`.

## Source

- Site: https://agentpassport.fyi
- GitHub: https://github.com/ceavinrufus/agentpassport
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/agentpassport/
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@agentpassport/core
